# Source Card: Reusable Assertion Ledger Phase 0 Fixture Metrics

## Summary

# Reusable Assertion Ledger Phase 0 deterministic fixture metrics This local-only source records the generated metrics and limitations of the frozen Phase 0 fixture.

## Key evidence

- (ev_001) # Reusable Assertion Ledger Phase 0 deterministic fixture metrics
- (ev_002) This local-only source records the generated metrics and limitations of the frozen Phase 0 fixture.
- (ev_003) The fixture safe reuse rate is 36 divided by 144, which equals 25.0 percent.
- (ev_004) The fixture provenance rate is 36 divided by 36, which equals 100.0 percent, with zero fixture provenance mismatches.
- (ev_005) The fixture baseline and replay values are 144 and 108 model calls, 720 and 576 elapsed fixture seconds, 144 and 108 cost units, and 72 and 54 review fixture minutes.
- (ev_006) The fixture rejection counts are 28 changed editions, 24 expired freshness states, 20 extraction-contract mismatches, 18 rights-or-sensitivity denials, and 18 evaluation-not-approved states.
- (ev_007) The fixture contains 12 retraction-propagation graphs and 120 expected affected objects, and it enumerates 120 of 120 objects.
- (ev_008) The fixture checks complete, duplicate, out-of-order, interrupted, and partial-resume delivery for all 12 invalidating fixture events against an independently authored expected-object manifest.

## Limitations

- Synthetic/local-only fixture evidence; it is not representative of a private corpus.
- Representative private-corpus economics, measured model cost, and reviewer effort are unavailable.
- Real-source normalization and canonical-merge safety are unavailable; no human merge labels were used.
